verdicts.note: +2 (fully 'refuse PAC money / reform the system') is exaggerated. The 'never taken corporate PAC money' claim is a single self-reported pamphlet line [S1], and the dossier's S4 (FEC) records $234,500 received from other political committees this cycle. She is also a nine-year incumbent with Progressive Caucus leadership and broad institutional labor/party endorsements [S1][S5] — textbook coalition-building within existing party structures (pole_a behavior). Genuine reform-bill leadership (stock-trading ban, campaign-finance reform) supports a pole_b lean, but +1 medium, not +2 high on S1 alone.

Score is sound, but the basis attributes the OneAmerica founding to S1/S3; in the dossier that claim is sourced to S2. The ICE bills [S3] and pamphlet anti-ICE/CBP statements [S1] independently carry the +2 high.

Direction is an indirect inference, not a clear statement. Her stated vehicle is to 'take over the Democratic Party first' [S1] — working through the existing party structure is the pole_a mechanism — and the dossier records no PAC-money refusal, term-limits, RCV, or other structural-reform plank. The +1 rests solely on anti-billionaire/anti-establishment rhetoric in one pamphlet statement; that is a low-confidence inference, not medium.

-2 (fully enforcement pole) overstates the evidence and ignores moderating material in the same sources. His pamphlet pairs 'law & order' with 'sustainable & affordable housing to solve homelessness' and 'Real Solutions to help homeless people' [S1] — a housing/services plank squarely on pole_b of this axis. Nothing in the dossier supports the harder pole_a markers (mandatory treatment, clearing encampments, prosecuting repeat offenders). Law-and-order-plus-fund-police as lead theme from an ex-officer earns a firm -1 at high confidence, not -2.

Medium overstates it. The axis is climate/carbon policy vs energy affordability; the dossier records only generic environmentalism ('environment #1 issue', 'Earth First', nature before corporate profits [S1][S2]) with no climate, carbon, or energy content at all — the scoring basis itself concedes this. Mapping generic pro-environment rhetoric onto this axis is an indirect inference: score +1 can stand only at low confidence.

Score stands, but note the tension in the basis: a perennial candidate running since 1999 [S3] is 'no record' rather than 'fresh perspective'; +2 is correct on the record-vs-no-record reading, and the citations support it.

missing.basis: The dossier records that she founded and co-chairs the Monopoly Busters Caucus 'to target consolidation and price-gouging' and leads bipartisan bills to 'limit government surveillance' [S1] — antitrust/platform-accountability and privacy-adjacent evidence pointing toward pole_b (regulate). No AI- or data-center-specific material, so low confidence only.
